title: Spent two days at the AI Agents Conference in NYC. Most of the companies there were betting on the wrong moat.
summary: Spent two days at the AI Agents Conference in NYC. Most of the companies there were betting on the wrong moat.
whyItMatters: One speaker (a VC) said his number for evaluating AI-native startups is ARR per engineer, and that the number ought to be going up. Almost every talk and every booth at the AI Agents Conference was selling a fix for something that broke this year when agents hit production. Observability, governance, supervisor agents, data substrates, "someone's gotta baby…
tags: Monitor only; do not publish as a standalone claim yet.
